name: Black Bean Chilaquile
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups corn kernels, fresh or frozen
1 1/2 cups black beans, cooked (15 oz can, drained)
2 cups swiss chard or spinach, rinsed, stemmed and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Saute the onions in oil for about 8 minutes until translucent.
3. Stir in tomatoes, corn, black beans, lime juice, salt, and pepper; continue sauteing another 5 to 10 minutes until heated through.
4. Blanch greens in boiling water (cover) for 1 to 3 minutes until just wilted but still bright green. Drain immediately.
5. Spread half the crushed tortilla chips on a prepared 8x8 inch casserole dish or baking pan coated lightly with oil or cooking spray.
6. Spoon sauteed vegetables over tortilla chips and sprinkle with about two-thirds of grated cheddar cheese.
7. Arrange greens evenly over cheese and spoon salsa on top.
8. Finish with remaining tortilla chips, then top with remaining salsa and cheddar.
9. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes until cheese is bubbling and beginning to brown.
